album.title: Songs From The Seaside
album.description:
LONGY AND THE GOSPEL TRASH
Liner Notes for Songs From The Seaside – Live in Southend”

There are live albums, and then there are documents. This is the latter.

It’s Songs From The Seaside Live in Southend captures Longy and the Gospel Trash at full tilt: 11 songs, a full big band, an upstairs pub in Southend, and a room thick with sweat, brass, electricity and belief. No studio polish. No safety net. Just wood floors, warm beer, overblown horns, and songs that have already lived a few lives before landing here.

Recorded in Longy’s hometown, upstairs in a pub where the ceiling feels low enough to lean on and the stage was never designed to hold that many players, this album isn’t about perfection. It’s about presence. You can hear the crowd pressed in close. You can hear glasses clink between verses. You can hear the band pushing air around the room like they’re trying to lift the roof clean off.

Longy and the Gospel Trash have always existed somewhere between sermon and bar fight. There’s gospel in the uplift, in the call-and-response spirit, in the sense that every chorus is meant to be sung by more than just the person holding the microphone. And there’s trash in the glorious refusal to tidy things up — in the ragged edges, the overdriven amps, the horn lines that sway instead of march.

For this recording, the songs were rebuilt for a big band setting. Brass punches through choruses like sunlight through pub windows at last orders. The rhythm section swings when it wants to, stomps when it needs to, and occasionally threatens to derail entirely before snapping back into place. It’s live music in its most honest form: slightly dangerous, deeply communal, completely unrepeatable.

These twelve songs feel less like a setlist and more like a reckoning. They carry hometown ghosts. They carry road miles. They carry laughter, stubborn hope, and a refusal to let small rooms shrink big feelings. If you’ve ever stood shoulder-to-shoulder with strangers and shouted a chorus back at a band because it felt necessary, you already know what this record is about.

Southend isn’t just the location — it’s part of the instrumentation. The salt air, the arcades, the stubbornness of a seaside town that keeps reinventing itself. Recording upstairs in a pub wasn’t a nostalgic choice. It was a statement: the songs belong to rooms like this. They belong to communities that show up.

And show up they did.

Published into the Valueverse

This album is published by the New Music Nudge Unit into the Valueverse — because how music is shared matters just as much as how it’s made.

The Valueverse is a growing digital ecosystem built around direct support, open networks, and artist sovereignty. Instead of relying on traditional streaming models where value trickles down through opaque systems, the Valueverse is designed around transparency, participation, and direct connection.

At the heart of it is V4V — Value for Value.

Value for Value is simple: if something moves you, you return value in whatever form you can. That might be money, time, attention, skills, amplification, or community. There are no paywalls forcing you in and no algorithms deciding what’s worthy. It’s voluntary, relational, and human.

Listeners become participants. Artists become nodes in a network rather than products in a catalogue. Support flows directly. Appreciation becomes tangible. Culture becomes collaborative again.

#demu
#v4v

This live album isn’t just a recording of twelve songs in an upstairs pub. It’s part of a wider movement experimenting with how art can circulate without losing its soul.

album.title: Healing Hands
album.description:
Healing Hands” enters the Valueverse as a quiet anthem of restoration and presence. With warmth and restraint, Longy delivers a song about the steady power of care — the kind that doesn’t shout, but lands.

Longy — the international musical street brawler from Southend-on-Sea, Essex — brings grit and soul in equal measure, blending lived experience with melodic honesty.

Released across open rails like Nostr, the track reflects a new era of independent music: direct, peer-to-peer, and powered by listeners who carry it forward.

Published by the New Music Nudge Unit.

album.title: Lady, You Shot Me!
album.description:
Longy steps into the Valueverse with a soulful cover of Sam Cooke’s “Lady, You Shot Me.”
A timeless song, reinterpreted with respect for the original and a sound shaped by today’s independent music rails. 

 

If you feel this one, boost it, zap it, and share it forward.
Music lives when people pass it hand to hand.

🎶 Original by Sam Cooke
🎤 Cover performed by Longy
⚡ Released into the Valueverse

#Valueverse #SamCooke #LadyYouShotMe #CoverSong #IndependentMusic 
#ZapTheMusic

This release is part of a wider move toward decentralising music through the Valueverse — a listener-supported ecosystem where fans directly support independent artists via value-for-value platforms.

publisherInfo.title: Longy - Everything
publisherInfo.description:
Longy is an Essex-born musician, songwriter and electrifying live performer known for his raw, genre-blending mix of rock, indie and punk-tinged storytelling. He first gained attention with his band Longy & The Gospel Trash, whose album Songs from the Seaside featured standout tracks including “Love Schizophrenia.” Alongside his band work, Longy has released a string of powerful singles such as “Tape Up,” “No One Goes Cold on My Watch,” “The Underclass,” “Sticky Boots,” “iMerica,” and “Back from the Abyss.”

A relentless touring artist, Longy has performed extensively across the UK, building a reputation for intense, emotionally charged live shows and appearances at independent festivals and headline venues. In 2025, he featured at Into the Valueverse, the UK’s first Bitcoin-powered live music event, where he was introduced to the value-for-value (V4V) model by the New Music Nudge Unit — marking a new chapter in his independent music journey.

album.title: Live at the Fickle Pickle - Into the Valueverse
album.description:
This is the live album exclusive connected to the show - Into the Valueverse - Remember this is a value for value album and the artists have put it out for free, so if you enjoyed this record? please send them some value back with a message and tell them who you are and where you are listening from. Bitcoin prefered! All of the artists and crew are #V4V enabled and almost all the value sent will go directly to them. 

Presented by the New Music Nudge Unit, Into the Valueverse was a groundbreaking live music event held on Friday, 23rd May 2025 at The Fickle Pickle Club in Westcliff-on-Sea, Essex.

As the UK’s first-ever Bitcoin-powered gig, it fused cutting-edge technology with raw, independent sound in an unforgettable night of borderless, peer-to-peer performance.

Live Stream Sound & Video: @iNPC

Sound Engineer for the room: Joe Lamb

Host and Exec Producer: Aaron Dorn (Aaron of Essex)

🎤 Lineup:

Joe Martin – Americana storyteller with soul

Setlist:
1. Checkmate
2. My Heart Decided For Me
3. High Gravity
4. The Jetsons
5. Crocodile Tears
6. Empty Passenger Seat


Nathan Abbott – Bluesy Essex heart with a rock edge
Drums: Archie
Bass: Andy Carr

Setlist:
1. The Big I Am
2. Jenny
3. Into the Light
4. Upside Down
5. Looking Glass
6. 50 - 1
7. Sweet Sunshine

Longy – Southend rebel with electrifying punk-folk fire
Drums - @Tommydrums

Set list:
1. Heroes/Tape Up
2. Love Schizophrenia
3. Promise to my people
4. Back from the Abyss
5. Penthouses and Pavements
6. Careful What You Wish For 
7. Don't get me Started


To access the valueverse download a modern podcast app such as https://Fountain.fm or listen in via your browser using https://lnbeats.com or https://curiocaster.com

The valueverse is where artists publish their music to the Bitcoin layer of the internet, allowing fans and listeners to send “boosts” (small Bitcoin payments) directly to them. It’s music in its purest form: peer-to-peer, value-for-value. Artists receive almost all of the value, with no middlemen — just music, connection, and freedom. If you want to know more then message aaron @ newmusicnudgeunit@gmail.com

All photography provided by the awesome Raj - https://www.rajeshtaylor.com/into-the-valueverse-fickle-pickle-club-westcliff-on-sea 

Show was supported by the legends at https://AZTE.co - Get your bitcoin in seconds!
https://fountain.fm - The podcast app which is revolutionising how we listen.

https://bitcoinessex.co.uk - Getting everyone in the room!
https://headstarts.uk - Making the idea become a reality.

album.title: Into the Valueverse - Full Show
album.description:
Presented by the New Music Nudge Unit, Into the Valueverse was a groundbreaking live music event held on Friday, 23rd May 2025 at The Fickle Pickle Club in Westcliff-on-Sea, Essex.
As the UK’s first-ever Bitcoin-powered gig, it fused cutting-edge technology with raw, independent sound in an unforgettable night of borderless, peer-to-peer performance.

Live Stream Sound & Video: @iNPC

Sound Engineer for the room: Joe Lamb

Host and Exec Producer: Aaron Dorn (Aaron of Essex)

🎤 Lineup:

Longy – Southend rebel with electrifying punk-folk fire
Drums - @Tommydrums

Joe Martin – Americana storyteller with soul

Nathan Abbott – Bluesy Essex heart with a rock edge
Drums: Archie
Bass: Andy Carr


Joe Martin's Set = time stamp - 00:05:00 - 00:35:00

1. Checkmate
2. My Heart Decided For Me
3. High Gravity
4. The Jetsons
5. Crocodile Tears
6. Empty Passenger Seat

Aaron's Rant = 00:35:13 - 00:38:20

Nathan Abbott's Set = time stamp - 00:38:40 - 01:09:29

1. The Big I Am
2. Jenny
3. Into the Light
4. Upside Down
5. Looking Glass
6. 50 - 1
7. Sweet Sunshine

Booster Bowie Re-cap = time stamp - 01:09:33 - 01:16:15

Longy's Set = time stamp - 01:16:16 - 01:53:50

1. Heroes/Tape Up
2. Love Schizophrenia
3. Promise to my people
4. Back from the Abyss
5. You Shine
6. Careful What You Wish For 
7. Don't get me Started


The show ran on Bitcoin’s value-for-value model, allowing fans to send real-time support — boosts and zaps — directly to the artists during their sets. It was livestreamed globally via Podcasting 2.0 apps and NOSTR Live, making it accessible to anyone, anywhere.

We raised over 0.0045 BTC (around £375 at the time) — all routed directly to the artists with no middlemen, no gatekeepers.

This recording captures the live show exactly as it happened, complete with value time splits — so when the artist changes, the bitcoin flows to the right wallet, seamlessly in the background.

All photos by https://www.rajeshtaylor.com/into-the-valueverse-fickle-pickle-club-westcliff-on-sea

🙏 Powered by:
Fountain.fm, Azte.co, RSS Blue, The Fickle Pickle, Bitcoin Essex, iNPC, and Headstarts.uk.

⚡ Music met money — and the crowd made it matter.

album.title: The Westend Sessions
album.description:
Artist: Edwin Williamson
Album: The Westend Sessions
Label: Incarnation Records
2022
Edwin Williamson’s unrestrained baritone recalls Scott Walker, blending rich melodies with a raw acoustic sensibility. His captivating songs, reminiscent of Nick Drake in their elemental lyricism, speak to urban wastelands peopled by poet junkies, gamblers, and femme fatales, all the while being tinged with a gritty world-weary swagger worthy of Johnny Cash or Nick Cave.
The Westend Sessions is the result of a collaboration between singer songwriter Astrid Williamson, Edwin’s sister, during the Covid19 lockdowns of 2020/21 and is released on Incarnation Records on 24th November 2022.
Released November 25, 2022
All tracks performed by Edwin Williamson and Astrid Williamson
Nicolas Dick — lap steel guitar on Veila
Nicky Smith — additional electric guitar on Veila
Engineered, mixed and produced by Astrid Williamson
Additional mixing on Dragon’s Eye by Nicky Smith
for more info go to https://bitcoinessex.co.uk/new-music-nudge-unit/
published on rss by nmnu new music nudge unit #demu #nmnu 

full details

Black Train

Counting House

Dragon’s Eye

Visions

Veila

Sunny 2nd Street 

City Of Light 

Outback

Morning Sun

Caramel 

My Dear

Nighttime

Carousel 


All songs written by Edwin Williamson except "Sunny 2nd Street", "My Dear", "Carousel" (Neil Cottee/Edwin Williamson), 'Nighttime", "Counting  House", "Morning Sun" (Dave Bernasconi). 


Lapsteel guitar on Veila performed by Nicholas Dick. 

Additional Guitars on Veila performed by Nicky Smith. 

Produced, engineered and mixed by Astrid Williamson.

Additional mixing on Dragon’s Eye and Veila by Nicky Smith.

Mastered by Nicholas Dick at Le Puits Sonore, Marseille.

Manufactured by AkcentMedia

Design by Kathryn Preston


P & C Incarnation Records 2022



Front and back cover artwork - Jacob Williamson



Paintings - Barbara Hulme 

Photography - Helen Davies

Special thanks to: Jake, Eddie & Linda Williamson, Nicky Smith, Nicolas Dick, Helen Davies, Dave Bernasconi and Barbara Hulme.

Dedicated to Neil Cottee.

1975 - 2017

"You'll never leave the weathered docks of our loving minds...

For you are in the moon, the wind and through the whole evening song you are the sailor in eternity whose journey is not gone.."

To support Edwin and buy the album in full go to https://incarnationrecords2020.bandcamp.com/album/the-westend-sessions

album.subtitle: Counting House
album.summary:
Artist: Edwin Williamson Track 1: Black Train Album: The Westend Sessions Label: Incarnation Records 2022 Producer: Astrid Williamson Composer: Edwin Williamson Lyricist: Edwin Williamson Edwin is also on Nostr - give him a follow
 
Artist: Edwin WilliamsonAlbum: The Westend SessionsLabel: Incarnation Records2022Edwin Williamson’s unrestrained baritone recalls Scott Walker, blending rich melodies with a raw acoustic sensibility. His captivating songs, reminiscent of Nick Drake in their elemental lyricism, speak to urban wastelands peopled by poet junkies, gamblers, and femme fatales, all the while being tinged with a gritty world-weary swagger worthy of Johnny Cash or Nick Cave.The Westend Sessions is the result of a collaboration between singer songwriter Astrid Williamson, Edwin’s sister, during the Covid19 lockdowns of 2020/21 and is released on Incarnation Records on 24th November 2022.Released November 25, 2022All tracks performed by Edwin Williamson and Astrid WilliamsonNicolas Dick — lap steel guitar on VeilaNicky Smith — additional electric guitar on VeilaEngineered, mixed and produced by Astrid WilliamsonAdditional mixing on Dragon’s Eye by Nicky Smithfor more info go to https://bitcoinessex.co.uk/new-music-nudge-unit/published on rss by nmnu new music nudge unit #demu #nmnu 
full details 
Edwin Williamson - The Westend Sessions 
Black Train
Counting House
Dragon’s Eye
Visions
Veila
Sunny 2nd Street 
City Of Light 
Outback
Morning Sun
Caramel 
My Dear
Nighttime
Carousel 
All songs written by Edwin Williamson except "Sunny 2nd Street", "My Dear", "Carousel" (Neil Cottee/Edwin Williamson), 'Nighttime", "Counting  House", "Morning Sun" (Dave Bernasconi). 
Lapsteel guitar on Veila performed by Nicholas Dick. 
Additional Guitars on Veila performed by Nicky Smith. 
Produced, engineered and mixed by Astrid Williamson.
Additional mixing on Dragon’s Eye and Veila by Nicky Smith.
Mastered by Nicholas Dick at Le Puits Sonore, Marseille.
Manufactured by AkcentMedia
Design by Kathryn Preston
P & C Incarnation Records 2022
 
Front and back cover artwork - Jacob Williamson
 
Paintings - Barbara Hulme 
Photography - Helen Davies
Special thanks to: Jake, Eddie & Linda Williamson, Nicky Smith, Nicolas Dick, Helen Davies, Dave Bernasconi and Barbara Hulme.
Dedicated to Neil Cottee.
1975 - 2017
"You'll never leave the weathered docks of our loving minds...
For you are in the moon, the wind and through the whole evening song you are the sailor in eternity whose journey is not gone.."
To support Edwin and buy the album in full go to https://incarnationrecords2020.bandcamp.com/album/the-westend-sessions

album.title: Good Enough (Single)
album.description:
Good Enough
Written and performed by Nathan Abbott

Produced, recorded, mixed, and mastered by Nathan Abbott

Published by The New Music Nudge Unit

Good Enough is a stripped-back reflection on self-worth, expectation, and the quiet work of learning to accept yourself without conditions. The production mirrors the song’s intent—unadorned, intimate, and deliberately honest—allowing the emotion to lead rather than the polish.

Nathan Abbott is an independent artist operating within the Valueverse, where listeners are invited to directly support the music and the creative process behind it. By choosing to engage in this way, fans play an active role in sustaining independent art beyond traditional industry models.

More information, music, and ways to support can be found at
nathanabbottmusic.co.uk

album.title: Circles
album.description:
In the heart of Southend, where seaworn concrete meets neon-lit promise, Hear My Roar emerged as a female-fronted tribe of misfits determined to carve out a sound that refuses to sit quietly in the corner. Their music is urgent, melodic, unapologetically loud—built from the grit of the coast, the pulse of the clubs, and the unshaken conviction that every voice deserves to be heard.

Now carried into the Valueverse by the New Mudge Unit, this release captures the band at a moment of raw ignition. Their songs surge forward with a blend of heavy hooks and atmospheric fire, each track shaped by the individual strengths of its members:

Kate West – vocals & acoustic guitar

[Member Name] – guitar

[Member Name] – bass

[Member Name] – drums

[Additional Member / Role]

United by a shared sense of purpose, they channel their varied backgrounds into something bigger than any single story—a collective roar that cuts through static and doubt alike.

Hear My Roar doesn’t just perform; they declare themselves. And with this publication into the Valueverse, the declaration only grows louder.

tracks.title: Brutal
tracks.subtitle: Brutal
tracks.summary:
Hailing from Southend, Hear My Roar are a female-fronted tribe of misfits who turn intensity into connection and volume into clarity. Their sound walks the line between melody and force, creating songs that feel both personal and confrontational.
“Brutal” marks another step in Hear My Roar’s journey into the Valueverse, published by the New Music Nudge Unit. It captures the band at their most honest—unpolished where it matters, powerful where it counts, and fully committed to making music that leaves a mark.

album.title: Run To Safer Ground (single) HEIDI J MASTERS
album.description:
Heidi J Masters is a UK singer-songwriter whose music blends heartfelt storytelling with powerful, emotive vocals. Her debut single Run to Safer Ground introduces listeners to a sound that is both intimate and anthemic, carrying themes of resilience, hope, and human connection. As part of the Valueverse, Heidi embraces the Value for Value model, releasing music directly to fans through open RSS feeds and value-enabled platforms like Fountain and LNbeats. With a voice that resonates across indie, folk, and pop audiences, Heidi J Masters is an artist to watch in 2025.

#demu #nmnu 

To learn more about the valueverse go to https://bitcoinessex.co.uk/new-music-nudge-unit/

album.title: Icarus
album.description: Jaded Jester are a fierce female-fronted alt-rock force emerging from Southend-on-Sea, blending hard-hitting nu-metal energy with emotionally charged songwriting led by multi-instrumentalist and vocalist Val Fida, alongside bassist Craig Lione and drummer Lawrence Faulkner; their sound fuses driving riffs, atmospheric textures and raw, introspective lyricism exploring resilience, transformation and unapologetic self-expression, capturing attention with powerful releases that showcase both grit and vulnerability, and now — officially entering the Valueverse — their music is published by New Music Nudge Unit, positioning the band at the forefront of a new decentralised era where artists and listeners connect directly through value-for-value support.

album.title: Singles
album.description: Frankie Peroni is a singer-songwriter based in the UK, originally from Essex (Westcliff-on-Sea). Frankie Peroni is an independent UK singer-songwriter from the Essex coast, crafting heartfelt pop with a DIY spirit and a storyteller’s edge. His music blends classic melodic songwriting with modern production, earning growing support across underground radio and online communities. Frankie is part of the evolving Valueverse music movement and is active on Nostr, connecting directly with fans while championing open, artist-first distribution.

tracks.title: How Will I Know What I Like (Until I Do It To You?)
tracks.subtitle: How Will I Know What I Like (Until I Do It To You?)
tracks.summary:
How Will I Know What I Like (Until I Do It To You?) is the debut single in the valueverse from Frankie Peroni.
A concise, melodic pop-rock track running roughly 3:07, the song showcases Frankie’s songwriting, vocals and acoustic-guitar foundation — setting the tone for the forthcoming album. Published by the New Music Nudge Unit. 
Frankie is also on Nostr - You should follow him. Frankie is also a resident DJ at Southend City Radio in Essex, UK.

album.title: Seaforth Road
album.description:
Frankie Peroni Is A Singer Songwriter From Westcliff On Sea In Essex UK.
"Seaforth Road" is Frankie Peroni’s most intimate release yet—an indie-rock-album soaked in nostalgia, evening streetlights, and the weight of places that never quite let you go. Across its tracks, Peroni reflects on the faces and memories that shaped him, blending soft storytelling with cinematic ambience. Honest, raw, and deeply human, this is an album about returning to where it all began—if only to finally move on.

publisherInfo.title: Frankie Peroni
publisherInfo.description:
Frankie Peroni – Artist Bio

Frankie Peroni is a singer-songwriter from Westcliff-on-Sea, Essex, known for crafting emotionally resonant indie-pop rooted in honest storytelling and collaboration. His catalogue blends reflective lyricism with melodic hooks, spanning standalone singles, creative reimaginations, and larger conceptual projects including Seaforth Road.

Introduced to the valueverse by the New Music Nudge Unit, Peroni quickly began building momentum, earning multiple podcast airplays and growing recognition within the UK independent scene. Constantly evolving, his work moves between intimate, stripped-back moments and expansive, idea-driven releases — always anchored by authenticity and a desire to connect.
